channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: US swaps prisoners with China, releasing 3 convicted spies
item.description: Two Chinese spies and a Chinese national charged for disseminating child pornography were part of a recent White House prison swap as Biden's presidency nears the end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European countries put freeze on Syrian asylum claims after Assad's fall, unclear if US will follow
item.description: European countries are freezing asylum cases from Syrian migrants after the ouster of Syrian President Bashar Assad, raising questions about how the U.S. will handle claims.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drone experts rule out US government experiment, unsure of other New Jersey drone phenomenon theories
item.description: Drone experts have little idea what the dozens of drone sightings over New Jersey could be, but have ruled out the possibility that they might be the work of a classified government program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: These are the Trump appointees who have faced legal troubles
item.description: President-elect Trump has named several people to his next administration who have faced legal troubles — including Trump himself, who was convicted in a New York hush money case during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Biden stirs outrage in Scranton by commuting 'kids for cash' judge's sentence
item.description: President Biden has stirred controversy after he commuted the sentence of a corrupt judge who was jailed for more than 17 years after he was caught taking kickbacks for sending juveniles to for-profit detention facilities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey drone sightings: Lawmakers press for more briefings
item.description: "Law enforcement and other agencies continue to support New Jersey and investigate the reports," the FBI said.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Dem claims Trump wielding nuclear strike authority 'should terrify you' — then people point out the obvious
item.description: Sen. Ed Markey, D-Mass., claims people should be terrified that President-elect Trump will have nuclear strike authority, but others pushed back, noting that Trump already had that authority during his first term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A nonprofit leader, a social worker: Here are the stories of the people on Biden's clemency list
item.description: Among the roughly 1,500 people whose sentences were commuted or who were pardoned by President Joe Biden are a nonprofit leader who supports at-risk youth, a social worker who fosters animals and a postdoctoral researcher One woman who received a pardon said she wanted people with drug addiction to
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top GOP rebel angles for powerful House leadership-backed committee post
item.description: Rep. Chip Roy is floating himself as a possible candidate to chair the influential House Rules Committee next year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mitt Romney's Senate exit may create a vacuum of vocal, conservative Trump critics
item.description: Sen. Mitt Romney has given his final remarks after seeing the Republican Party for which he was once the standard bearer drastically transform under Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Watchdog finds FBI intelligence missteps before Jan. 6 riot, but no undercover agents were present
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI should have done more to gather intelligence before the Capitol riot, according to a watchdog report Thursday that also said no undercover FBI employees were on the scene on Jan. 6, 2021, and none of the bureau’s informants was authorized to participate.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden administration makes final diplomatic push for stability across a turbulent Mideast
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — Top U.S. officials were in the Middle East on Thursday, pushing for stability in Syria and an end to Israel’s 14-month war in the Gaza Strip in a last-ditch diplomatic push by the outgoing Biden administration before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in a few weeks.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Senate chaplain Barry Black hospitalized after brain bleed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. Senate Chaplain Barry Black has been hospitalized after suffering a bleed on his brain and is expected to have a “smooth recovery,” his office said.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hill to chair House Financial Services Committee, Walberg to lead Education and Workforce panel
item.description: House Republican leaders on Thursday choose Rep. French Hill (R-Ark.) to lead the House Financial Services Committee in the 119th Congress and Rep. Tim Walberg (R-Mich.) to chair the House Committee on Education and the Workforce, completing their selections for committee chairs.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-FBI informant accused of lying about Bidens agrees to plead guilty to federal charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A former FBI informant accused of falsely claiming that President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepted bribes has agreed to plead guilty to federal charges, according to court papers filed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford exploring run for governor in 2026
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford is exploring a run to become his party's nominee for governor in 2026.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: “I think if President Biden wanted to talk to me about that, I will talk to him about it, but I don’t think I should be giving public advice on the pardon power,” Bill Clinton said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year and rings the New York Stock Exchange's opening bell
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump rang the opening bell Thursday at the New York Stock Exchange after being recognized for the second time by Time magazine as its person of the year.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. wants daughter-in-law in CIA top role to probe JFK assassination
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. reportedly wants Amaryllis Fox Kennedy and Trump to help release documents about the JFK assassination.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump mass deportation threats infuriate immigrant advocates
item.description: President-elect Trump’s pledge to enact mass deportations and end birthright citizenship, potentially affecting millions of U.S. citizens, is raising alarm bells among immigrant communities who fear an overzealous Trump administration could destroy countless families.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: FAA administrator to resign on Trump swearing-in
item.description: Mike Whitaker is leaving at the start of President-elect Trump’s term, a source familiar with his plans told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences for 1,500 people, the largest act of clemency in a day
item.description: The 1,500 people had been serving long prison sentences that would have been shorter under today's laws and practices. Three Minnesota women are among those to get their federal convictions pardoned.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith and Chandra Colvin, MPR News

item.title: Q&A: Ben Wikler running for DNC chair
item.description: Ben Wikler is chair of the Democratic Party of Wisconsin and candidate for chair of the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Q&A: Martin O’Malley running for DNC chair
item.description: Martin O’Malley is a former governor of Maryland and candidate for chair of the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Christopher Wray to resign as FBI director ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray announced Wednesday he plans to resign from the agency, leaving his post ahead of President-Trump's apparent plans to fire him.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's withdrawn nominees latest in a long line of Cabinet members who never were
item.description: Many presidents face failed Cabinet picks so when former Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew from consideration for President-elect Donald Trump's Cabinet, it wasn't unprecedented, though Trump's style has been.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Florida State Rep. Susan Valdes, representing parts of Hillsborough County, switches political parties
item.description: State Rep. Susan Valdes, who represents Florida's 64th District, including parts of Hillsborough County, switched political parties, the Florida House of Representatives announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump return to DC looms over Kennedy Center Honors
item.description: In a night filled with political moments — including multiple nods to President Joe Biden, a Grateful Dead tribute from former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5
